verses.text: Ziba meets the fleeing king with donkeys and food, and slanders his master Mephibosheth as a traitor hoping for the throne. David, weary and unable to check the tale, gives Ziba all the land. Haste in trouble believes the first story; a one-sided accusation is dangerous to judge by.

As David passes, Shimei of Saul's house curses him and pelts him with stones, calling him a bloody man getting his due. The fallen king must bear public scorn. When we are down, old enemies feel free to speak, and their cruelty adds to the weight of the hour.

Abishai offers to take off Shimei's head, but David restrains him: 'let him curse... it may be the LORD will look on mine affliction, and requite me good.' He receives the abuse as from God's hand, hoping for mercy. Humility under reproach leaves room for grace.

Abishai offers to take off Shimei's head, but David restrains him: 'let him curse... it may be the LORD will look on mine affliction, and requite me good.' He receives the abuse as from God's hand, hoping for mercy. Humility under reproach leaves room for grace.

David and his people go on their weary way, Shimei still cursing alongside, and rest at last, exhausted. The road of the chastened is long and hard. Yet they keep moving forward, and even the afflicted find a place to pause and refresh under God's care.

Absalom enters Jerusalem in triumph, and Hushai greets him, 'God save the king,' feigning loyalty to David's friend. The deceiver is planted in the rebel's court, as David prayed. God answers the prayers of His people through the quiet courage of those willing to serve in danger.

Ahithophel advises Absalom to lie with David's concubines in the sight of all Israel — fulfilling to the letter Nathan's word that David's sin would be repaid openly. The counsel men prize as wisdom serves God's foretold judgment. His word stands, however dark the means.
